Record.statement_en: D/b base quantities and derived quantities? Give three examples .
Record.statement_ur: بنیادی مقداروں اور ماخوذ مقداروں میں کیا فرق ہے؟ تین مثالیں دیجیے۔
Record.answer_en: There are seven physical quantities that provide the basis for all other quantities. These quantities are called fundamental quantities. For example, length, mass, time, temperature, intensity of light, electric current and quantities of matter are basic quantities. Whereas all such quantities which are derived from the fundamental quantities are called derived quantities. For example Velocity, Area and Volume etc.

Record.statement_en: Why do we need to measure extremely small interval of time?
Record.statement_ur: ہمیں وقت کے انتہائی قلیل وقفوں کو ماپنے کی ضرورت کیوں پڑتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: In scientific experiments we measure very short intervals of time to make accurate measurements. For example, the time period of a helical spring, the time period of a simple pendulum, the time of falling of a bullet in a free fall experiment is very short. Therefore, a stopwatch is used to measure such times.

Record.statement_en: How is precision related to the significant figures in a measured quantity?

Record.answer_en: The exactness of the measured quantity depends on quite a few significant figures. For example, the length of a rod measured with a meter rod is 5 cm, while the same rod with a vernier caliper measures 5.02 cm, so the measurement taken with a vernier caliper is more accurate.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between scalar and vectors.
Record.statement_ur: سکیلر اور ویکٹر میں فرق بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Scaler quantities: Vector quantities
Those physical quantities that we express completely by their quantity are called scalars. Mass Temperature and Speed â€‹â€‹are scalar quantities Physical quantities that require both magnitude and direction to describe such as velocity, acceleration, and force are called vectors.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between circular motion and rotatory motion ?
Record.statement_ur: سرکلر موشن اور روٹیٹری موشن میں فرق بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Circular motion Rotatory motion
The motion of a body in a circle is called circular motion. The motion of a body around its axis is called rotary motion.
The movement of the earth around the sun is an example of circular motion. The movement of the earth around its axis is called rotary motion.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between distance and displacement.
Record.statement_ur: فاصلہ اور ڈس پلیسمنٹ میں فرق بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Distance Displacement
The length of the path between two points is called the distance between them. The minimum distance between two points is called displacement.
Distance is a scalar quantity. Displacement is a vector quantity.

Record.answer_en:
Linear motion Random motion
Motion of a body in a straight line is called linear motion. An airplane flying in a straight line and objects falling vertically are examples of Le Noir motion. The random motion of a body is called random motion.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between rest and motion?
Record.statement_ur: ریسٹ اور موشن میں فرق بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Rest Motion
If a body is not changing its position with respect to its surroundings, it is said to be at rest. If a body is changing its position depending on its angular position, it is said to be in motion
If a book is placed on the table, it is at rest. A train leaving a station is in motion according to people standing on the platform.

Record.statement_en: How are vector quanties important to us in our daily life?
Record.statement_ur: روزمرہ زندگی میں ویکٹر مقداروں کی اہمیت بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Vector quantities are used in everyday life for various purposes and functions. Describing a vector without a direction is meaningless. For example, the distance of a location from a reference point is insufficient to identify that location. At this point, the knowledge of the direction from the reference point is also very important, as its direction is also known in daily life, then vector quantities play an important role in this regard.

Record.statement_en: Why does a passenger move outward when a bus takes a turn?

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between action and reaction.
Record.statement_ur: ایکشن اور ری ایکشن میں فرق واضح کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Action Reaction
A force exerted on a body is called action. The force which is applied in the opposite direction of action is called reaction. The magnitude of this force is equal to the action.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between torque and couple.
Record.statement_ur: ٹارک اور کپل میں فرق کی واضح کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Torque couple
The effect of rotating a force is called torque. In mathematical terms, the product of moment arm and force is called torque. When two equal and unlike parallel forces act on a body and produce a torque in it, it is called a couple.

Record.statement_en: Differentiate between stable and neutral equilibrium.
Record.statement_ur: قیام پذیر اور نیوٹرل ایکوی لبریم میں فرق واضح کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
Stable Equilibrium Neutral Equilibrium
If a body is slightly moved from its equilibrium position and its center of gravity is raised than before, then the body returns to its original position. Such an equilibrium state of the body is called a stable equilibrium. If a body is moved slightly from its equilibrium position and its center of gravity does not change, the body assumes a new position. Such an equilibrium condition is called neutral equilibrium.

Record.statement_en: Why there is a need of second condition of equilibrium if a body satisfied first condition of equilibrium?
Record.statement_ur: ایکوی لبریم کی دوسری شرط کی کیا ضرورت ہے اگر کوئی جسم ایکوی لبریم کی پہلی شرط پوری کرتا ہے؟
Record.answer_en: When two equal and opposite forces act on a body at two different points, the total force on the body is zero, but the body is not in equilibrium and the body tends to rotate despite the fulfillment of the first condition. Here, for the body to be on the equilabrium, the second condition must also be fulfilled, i.e., for the body to be completely in the equilabrium, the net force on it must be zero and the net torque must also be zero.

Record.statement_en: What is meant by force of gravitation?

Record.answer_en: Some of the salient features of kinetic molecular models of matter are as follows. 1) Matter is made up of particles called molecules. 2) Molecules are constantly moving. 3) There is a force of attraction between molecules. This theory of kinetic molecules describes three states of matter: solid, liquid and gas.

Record.statement_en: Does there exist a fourth state of  matter? What is that?
Record.statement_ur: کیا مادہ کی چوتھی حالت پائی جاتی ہے؟ اگر ہاں تو وہ کونسی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Plasma is the fourth state of matter. Whenever a gas is continuously heated, the kinetic energy of the gas molecules increases, due to which the atoms and molecules collide with each other and the gas atoms start breaking. Electrons of atoms get separated and atoms become positive ions, this state of matter is called plasma.

Record.statement_en: Show that atmosphere exerts pressure.

Record.answer_en: Take an empty tin with a lid. Remove the lid and add little water and heat it on high flame. Until the water boils and the steam expels the air in the can, take it off the fire and close the lid of the can tightly and when we put it under tap water, the can will be cooked. Because the steam in the can will cool the water. It freezes due to water vapor and the pressure inside the can is reduced from the outside atmospheric pressure, due to which the can collapses on all sides.

Record.statement_en: Why water is not suitable to be used in a barometer?

Record.answer_en: Mercury is 13.6 times denser than water, so the atmospheric pressure can support a column of water 13.6 times higher than the column of mercury at some point. Therefore, the vertical height of the water column at sea level is 0.76m × 13.6 = 10.34m.So a glass tube more than 10m long would be required to make a water barometer.

Record.statement_en: What is Hook's law? What is meant by elastic limit?
Record.statement_ur: ہک کا قانون کیا ہے؟ ایلاسٹک لمٹ سے کیا مراد ہے؟
Record.answer_en:
The strain produced in any body within the elastic limit is directly proportional to the stress applied to it.
Hooke's law applies to deformation within the elastic limit in all types of materials. Elastic limit: Elastic limit is the limit within which the body returns to its original length, volume or shape when the deforming force is removed.

Record.answer_en: Heat always flows from a hot body to a cold body. This heat flow is due to the difference in temperature and this heat flow is always from higher temperature to lower temperature.

Record.statement_en: Why metals are good conductors of  heat?
Record.statement_ur: میٹلز اچھی کنڈیکٹر کیوں ہوتی ہیں؟
Record.answer_en: Metals have free electrons which move at a very high speed all the time in metals and due to their high speed they transfer heat very quickly from hot to cold parts thus metals are better conductors than non-metals.

Record.statement_en: Explain why metal feels colder to touch than wood ?
Record.statement_ur: وضاحت کریں کہ کیوں چھونے سے ٹھنڈی جگہ پر پڑی میٹل کی شے بہ نسبت لکٹری کے زیادہ ٹھنڈی محسوس ہوتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Wood, being an insulator, transfers heat at a slower rate, while metals, being conductors, transfer heat at a faster rate. This is why a wooden object in a cold place feels less cold than metal.

Record.statement_en: Explain why land breeze blows from  land towards sea?
Record.statement_ur: وضاحت کریں کہ کیوں نسیم بری خشکی سے سمندر کی جانب چلتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: At night, the land cools faster than the ocean. Therefore, the air over the ocean rises due to being relatively warmer and cool air from the land moves towards the ocean to take its place.

Record.answer_en: For the process of conduction, materials need to be good conductors, in which heat is transferred to molecules and electrons at high speed through the process of conduction. Whereas in gases, heat transfer occurs by convection because gases are poor conductors.

Record.statement_en: What is meant by convection  current ?
Record.statement_ur: کنویکشن کرنٹس کا کیا مطلب ہے؟
Record.answer_en: Liquids and gases are poor conductors that become lighter when heated and rise to be replaced by cooler gases or liquids. The rate of flow of liquids and gases is called convection currents. Naseem Bahri and Naseem Bari are examples of convection currents.

Record.statement_en: Suggest a simple activity to show convection of heat in gases not given in the book.

Record.answer_en: In a geyser boiler used in homes, the water is heated and rises up by the process of convection. In its place, cold water enters the boiler. In this, the hot water comes out from the upper part of the tank. While the cold water pipe enters from the bottom of the boiler.

Record.statement_en: Define the terms speed, velocity, and acceleration?
Record.statement_ur: سپیڈ ، ولاسٹی اور ایکسلریشن کی تعریف کیجئے ؟
Record.answer_en:
Speed: The distance traveled by a body in unit time is called its speed. Its unit is meter per second.
Velocity: The rate of change of displacement of a body is called velocity. The unit of velocity is meter per second per second.
Acceleration: The rate of change in velocity of a body is called acceleration. The unit of acceleration is meter per second.

Record.statement_en: How is energy converted from one form to another? Explain.
Record.statement_ur: انرجی کی ایک شک سے دوسری شکل میں کیسے تبدیل کیا جاتاہے ؟ وضاحت کیجئے ۔
Record.answer_en: The heat energy from the sun is absorbed by the water in some oceans, which causes an increase in thermal energy, which causes the water to turn into vapor, and this vapor rises to form clouds, and when these clouds cool When it reaches the areas, it takes the form of rain. In this way, the potential energy is converted into kinetic energy and when this water flows in low-lying areas, the kinetic energy is converted into mechanical energy.

Record.statement_en: Explain different types of translatory  motion with examples.
Record.statement_ur: ٹرانسلیٹری موشن کی مختلف اقسام کی مثالیں دے کر وضاحت کریں۔
Record.answer_en:
There are three types of translational motion (1) Leigh motion (2) Circular motion (3) Random motion.
Linear Motion: “The motion of a body in a straight line is called Leniar Motion. A plane moving in a straight line. A body coming towards the earth or a plane flying are examples of Leniar Motion.
Circular Motion: “The movement of a body in a circle is called circular motion. The rotation of the earth around the sun and the rotation of the moon around the earth are examples of circular motion.
Random Motion: "The random motion of a body is called random motion. The motion of insects and birds is random motion."

Record.statement_en: Describes ways  to reduce Friction.
Record.statement_ur: فرکشن کو کم کرنے کےلیے طریقے بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Fraction can be reduced in the following ways: (1) If we coordinate the moving surfaces on each other. (2) Apply oil or grease between metal parts. (3) Sliding friction can be reduced by changing it to rolling friction, so ball bearings or roller bearings are used.

Record.statement_en: Write the difference between sliding fraction and rolling fraction.
Record.statement_ur: سلائڈنگ فرکشن اور رولنگ فرکشن کے درمیان فرق لکھیں۔
Record.answer_en:
Sliding friction  Rolling friction
When one surface is dragged over another, the force that resists the motion of one surface on another surface is called sliding friction. Sliding fraction is much higher than rolling fraction. When a body is moved with the help of a wheel, the force of friction is called rolling friction. Rolling fraction is much less than sliding fraction.

Mass Weight
The amount of matter of a body is called the mass of the body. Weight is the gravitational force acting on a body.
It is a scalar quantity. It is a vector quantity.
Its unit is kg. weight unit is Newton.

Record.answer_en: The head total rule method is used to sum vectors by graph method. In this, the head of the first vector is combined with the tail of the second vector and the tail of the first vector is combined with the head of the last vector to find the resultant.

Record.statement_en: What is meant by stable and non-stable and neutral equilibrium?
Record.statement_ur: قیام پذیر اور غیر قیام پذیر اور نیوٹرل ایکوی لبریم سے کیامرادہے؟
Record.answer_en: Resting Equilibrium: If a body is lifted and released and it returns to its original state, then this state of the body is called resting state. In this, the body's speed of gravity increases. If the book lying on the table is lifted a little and left, it will return to its first state. Such a state is called the resting state. Unstable: If a body does not return to its original position after being slightly distorted, then such a state of the body is called an unsettled state. It lowers the center of gravity of the body. If the pencil is placed on its tip, dropping the pencil will cause the pencil to fall and not return to its original position. Neutral Equilibrium: If a body stays in its original state after being shaken from its initial state, then this state of the body is called neutral equilibrium. In this the center of gravity of the body is neither high nor low. If the football is moved a little on a horizontal surface, it will go a little further and come into equilibrum, this is called neutral equilibrium.

Record.statement_en: Explain Newton's law of gravitation.
Record.statement_ur: نیوٹن کے گریوی ٹیشن کے قانون کی وضاحت کیجئے۔
Record.answer_en: Every body in the universe attracts other bodies with a force and this force is directly proportional to the masses of the bodies and inversely proportional to the distance between the centers of the bodies.

Record.statement_en: Define latent heat of fusion.
Record.statement_ur: پگھلاؤ کی مخفی حرارت کی تعریف کیجیے۔
Record.answer_en: The thermal energy required to change unit mass of a solid from solid to liquid at its melting point without changing its temperature is called its latent heat of fusion. It is denoted by Hf.

Record.statement_en: Define specific heat. How is the specific heat of a solid body determined?
Record.statement_ur: حرارت مخصوصہ کی تعریف کیجیے۔ ایک ٹھوس جسم کی حرارت مخصوصہ کیسے معلوم کی جاتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The specific heat of a body is the amount required to cause a one-kilogram change in one kilogram of its mass.

Record.statement_en: What is meant by evaporation? Evaporation of a liquid depends on what factors? Clarify. How does evaporation produce cooling?
Record.statement_ur: ایویپوریشن سے کیا مراد ہے؟ کسی مائع کی ایوپوریشن کا انحصار کن عوامل پر ہوتاہے؟ واضح کریں۔ ایویپوریشن سے ٹھنڈک کیسے پیداہوتی ہے؟
Record.answer_en: The vaporization of a liquid from the surface of a liquid without heating it. Evaporation depends on the following factors. 1) Temperature 2) Surface pressure 3) Air velocity 4) Nature of liquid Cooling by evaporation: By evaporation, molecules with high kinetic energy leave the surface of the liquid and molecules with low kinetic energy remain on the surface of the liquid. As the temperature depends on the kinetic energy of the molecules. Due to low kinetic energy, the temperature also decreases and cooling occurs.

Record.statement_en: Explain thermal diffusion in volume.
Record.statement_ur: والیوم میں حرارتی پھیلاؤ کی وضاحت کریں۔
Record.answer_en: When objects are heated, their volume increases, this is called thermal expansion in volume. The volume expansion of a body depends on the change in its original volume and temperature.

Record.statement_en: Why might a standard system of measurement be helpful to a tailor?
You are provided a metre scale and a bundle of pencils ; how can the diameter of a pencil be measured using the metre scale with the same precision as that of Vernier Callipers? Describe briefly.
The end of a metre scale is worn out. Where will you place a pencil to find the length?
Why is better to place the object close to the metre scale?
Why a standard unit is needed to measure a quantity correctly?
Suggest some natural phenomena that could save as a reasonably accurate time standard.
It is difficult to locate the meniscus in a wider vessel . Why?
Distance and displacement may or may not be equal in magnitude. Explain this statement.

When a bullet is fired, its velocity with which it leaves the barrel is called the muzzle velocity of the gun. The muzzle velocity of one gun with a longer barrel is lesser than that of another gun with a shorter barrel. In which gun is the acceleration of the bullet lager? Explain your answer.

For a complete trip, average velocity was calculated. Its value came out to be positive. Is it possible that its instantaneous valocity at any time during the trip had the negative value? Give justification of your answer.
Is it possible that the velocity of an object is zero at an instant of time, but its acceleeration is not zero? If yes, give an example of such a case.

Two ice skaters weighing 60kg and 80kg push off against each other on a frictionless ice track. The 60kg skater gains a velocity of 4 m s<sup>-1</sup> . Considering all the relevant calculations involved, explain how Newton's third law applies to this situation.
Inflatable airbags are installed in the vehicles as safety equipment. In terms of momentum, what is the advantage of airbags over seatbelts?

A horse refuses to pull a cart. The horse argues, "according to Newton's third law, whatever force I exert on the cart, the cart will exert an equal and opposite force on me. Since the net force will be xero, therfore, I have no chance of accelerating (pulling) the cart." What is wrong with this reasoning?
When a cricket ball hits high, a fielder tries to catch it. While holding the ball he/she draws hands backward. Why?
When someone jumps from a small boat onto the river bank, why does the jumper often fall into the water? Explain.
Imagine that if friction vanishes suddenly from everthing, then what could be the scenario of daily life activities?
A car travels at the same speed around two curves with different radii. For which radius the car experiences more centripetal force? Prove your answer.
A ripe mango does not normally fall from the tree. But when the branch of the tree is shaken, the mango falls down easily. Can you tell the reason?

Discuss the concepts of stability and centre of gravity in relation to objects toppling over. Provide an example where an object's cantre of gravity affects its stability, and explain how altering its base of support can influence stability.
Why an accelerated body cannot be considered in equilibrium?
Two boxes of the same weight but different heights are lying on the floor of a truck. If the truck makes a sudden stop, which box is more likely to tumble over? Why?
Can the kinetic energy of a body ever be negative?
Which one has the greater kinetic energy; an object travelling with a velocity v or an object twice as heavy travelling with a velocity of  1/2  v?
A car is moving along a curved road at constant speed. Does its kinetic energy change?
Comment on the statement. " An object has one joule of potential energy."
While driving on a motorway, tyre of a vehicle sometimes bursts. What may be its cause?
While playing cricket on a street, the ball smashes a window pane. Describe the energy changes in this event.
A man rowing boat upstream is at rest with respect to the shore. Is he doing work?

A cyclist goes downhill from the top of a steep hill without pedalling and takes it to the top of the next hill. 
(i) Draw a diagram of what happened.
(ii) Analyse this event in terms of potential and kinetic energy. 
Label your diagram using these terms.
Is timber or wood renewable source of heat energy? Comment.
Springs are made of steel instead of iron. Why?

Which of the following material is more elastic? 
(a) Iron or rubber                       (b) Air or water
How does water pressure one metre below the surface of a swimming pool compare to water pressure one metre below the surface of a very large and deep lake?
What will happen to the pressure in all parts of a confined liquid if pressure is increased in one part? Give an example from your daily life where such principle is applied.
If some air remains trapped within the top of the mercury column of the barometer which is supposed to be vacuum ,  how would it affect the height of the mercury column?
The end of glass tube used in a simple barometer is not properly sealed, some leak is present. What will be its effect?
Comment on the statement. " Density is a property of a material not the property of an object made of that material".
How the load of a large structure is estimated by an engineer?
Is kinetic molecular theory of matter applicable to the plasma state of matter? Describe briefly.
Why is mercury usually preferred to alcohol as a thermometric liquid?
Why is water not suitable for use in thermometers? Without calculations, guess what is equivalent temperature of 373 K on Celsius and Fahrenhit scales?
Mention two ways in which the design of a liquid - in - glass thermometer may be altered to increase its sensitivity.
One litre of water is heated by a stove and its temperature rises by 2<sup>o</sup>C. If two litres of water is heated on the same stove for the same time, what will be then rise in temperature?
Why are there no negative numbers on the Kelvin scale?
Comment on the statement. " A thermometer measures its own temperature."
There are various objects made of cotton, wood, plastic, metals, etc. In a winter night, compare their temperatures with the air temperature by touching them with your hand.
Which is greater : an increase in temperature 1<sup>o</sup> C or one 1<sup>o</sup>F ?
Why would not you expect all the molecules in a gas to have the same speed?
Does it make sense to talk about the temperature of a vacuum?
Comment on the statement: " A hot body does not contain heat".
Discuss whether the Sun is matter.

Electric current or motion of electrons produce magnetic field. Is the reverse process true, that is the magnetic field gives rise to electric current? If yes, give an example and describe it briefly.
Is the theory of science an ultimate truth? Describe briefly.
Do you think that the existing laws of nature may need a change in future? Describe briefly.
Describe three jobs that need the use of scientific knowledge.
Describe when a theory is rejected or needs its modification.
Comment on the statement. "A theory capable of being proved right but not being proved wrong is not a scientific theory."
What has been the general reaction to new ideas about established truths?
If a hypothesis not testable, is the hypothesis wrong? Explain.
Explain how a small amount of data cannot prove that a prediction is always correct but can prove it is not always correct.
What is the relation between an experiment and a hypothesis?
Describe why the solution of complex problems need interdisciplinary research and collaboration.
